content.problemStatement:
A tuple (a,b,c)(a, b, c)(a,b,c) is considered good if it consists of three **prime numbers** aaa, bbb and ccc such that a<b<c≤Na \lt b \lt c \leq Na<b<c≤N and a+b=ca + b = ca+b=c.

Two tuples are considered different if they differ in at least one position. Given NNN, find the number of good tuples that can be formed by integers from 111 to NNN.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The following TTT lines contain a single integer NNN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the number of good tuples which can be formed using integers from 111 to NNN.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: There are no good tuples. (1,2,3)(1, 2, 3)(1,2,3) is not a good tuple since 111 is not prime.
Example case 2: (2,3,5)(2, 3, 5)(2,3,5) is a good tuple since 222, 333 and 555 are prime and 2+3=52 + 3 = 52+3=5.
